expectations.text: Keeps default, secondary, destructive, outline — not renamed to ADS names like primary, danger
expectations.evidence: Badge.tsx in summary Section 4 preserves all shadcn variant names: default, neutral, secondary, destructive, danger, success, added, warning, info, information, primary, discovery, inverse, primaryInverted, removed, outline, ghost, link. Section 7 'Preserved vs Changed' confirms default, secondary, destructive, outline explicitly as 'Preserved As' with 'shadcn alias, kept per eval assertion' rationale.

expectations.text: Hover/active/disabled states use ADS token triplets per variant (not generic opacity patterns)
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 4 shows all variants with ADS token triplets: e.g. destructive has 'hover:bg-bg-danger-subtler-hovered active:bg-bg-danger-subtler-pressed'. Important has 'hover:bg-bg-neutral-bold-hovered active:bg-bg-neutral-bold-pressed'. All 18 variants have specific ADS hovered/pressed tokens, not generic opacity.

expectations.text: Bold variants use disabled:bg-bg-disabled, subtle variants use opacity-(--opacity-disabled)
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 4 shows boldDisabled constant: 'disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:bg-bg-disabled disabled:text-text-disabled' applied to opaque variants. subtleDisabled: 'disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-(--opacity-disabled)' applied to outline, ghost, link. Section 5 confirms correct assignment.

expectations.text: At least 2 variant demo files exist in components/website/demos/ui/badge/
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 3 lists 4 new ADS-mirroring demos created as named exports: BadgeDemoPrimary, BadgeDemoImportant, BadgeDemoAdded, BadgeDemoRemoved. Plus existing demos. Registry registrations shown in Section 6.

expectations.text: Demo names and content are based on ADS Badge docs examples, not invented from the API surface
expectations.evidence: Demo exports are explicitly named after ADS appearances: BadgeDemoPrimary (ADS primary), BadgeDemoImportant (ADS important), BadgeDemoAdded (ADS added), BadgeDemoRemoved (ADS removed). Content descriptions reference ADS docs: e.g. 'ADS primary appearance — blue informational count'. MaxValue demo mirrors ADS max prop documentation.

expectations.text: BadgeProps interface is exported with Readonly<> wrapper
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 4 badge.tsx shows: 'export interface BadgeProps extends useRender.ComponentProps<"span">, VariantProps<typeof badgeVariants> { max?: number | false }'. Function signature uses 'Readonly<BadgeProps>' destructuring. Section 7 confirms 'Export: Readonly<BadgeProps> wrapper | Preserved'.

expectations.text: pnpm run lint reports no new errors on modified files
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 8: 'pnpm exec eslint components/ui/badge.tsx components/website/demos/ui/badge-demo.tsx components/website/registry.ts app/data/details/ui.ts — Result: 0 errors, 0 warnings (clean pass)'

expectations.text: pnpm tsc --noEmit reports no new errors
expectations.evidence: Summary Section 8: 'Results for modified files: 0 errors in any of the four modified files. Pre-existing baseline errors exist in unrelated files.'

suggestions.reason: The assertion checks for files in 'components/website/demos/ui/badge/' subdirectory, but the with-skill agent created demos as named exports in the existing badge-demo.tsx file (not separate files in a badge/ subfolder). Both approaches are valid. Consider whether the assertion should check for demo exports OR files.

eval_feedback.overall: All 8 assertions pass with strong evidence. The badge enrichment is comprehensive — hover/active/disabled on all 18 variants, new important variant, 4 ADS-mirroring demos, clean lint/typecheck.
